note: Static generic base reference, hand-editable and version-controlled. write_rubric() loads this, computes per-user baselines from observations.jsonl, and writes the merged full_reference.json for handoff. This base file is never auto-overwritten. It is a reference scale, not an observation: read a metric value together with the matching entry to interpret it.

direction_legend.higher_is_better: above the range/baseline is the favorable direction
direction_legend.lower_is_better: below the range/baseline is the favorable direction
direction_legend.neutral: not good or bad on its own; interpret with related metrics
direction_legend.stable_is_normal: steady is normal; deviations either way are notable

whoop.strain.label: WHOOP strain
whoop.strain.note: Cumulative running total for a WHOOP cycle (wake-to-wake); take the latest value, do not sum updates. High strain is only 'bad' relative to low recovery.

whoop.recovery_score.label: WHOOP recovery
whoop.recovery_score.note: Computed once each morning and final for the cycle; already normalized to the individual.

whoop.sleep_performance.label: WHOOP sleep performance
whoop.sleep_performance.note: Share of the night's sleep need that was met; higher is better.

oura.score.label: Oura readiness / sleep / activity score
oura.score.note: Daily activity and stress totals are cumulative for the Oura day (take latest, not summed).

whoop.hrv_rmssd.label: WHOOP HRV (RMSSD)
whoop.hrv_rmssd.note: HRV rises with parasympathetic (rest/recovery) activity and fitness; higher than baseline = better recovered, a drop signals stress, fatigue, or illness. Opposite direction from resting heart rate. Measured by WHOOP during sleep, so keep its baseline separate from Oura HRV.

whoop.resting_heart_rate.label: WHOOP resting heart rate
whoop.resting_heart_rate.note: Lower than baseline = better recovered; an elevation signals stress, illness, or under-recovery.

whoop.respiratory_rate.label: WHOOP respiratory rate
whoop.respiratory_rate.note: Usually steady night to night; a sustained rise can indicate illness or strain.

whoop.skin_temp_celsius.label: WHOOP skin temperature
whoop.skin_temp_celsius.note: Deviations from baseline can indicate illness or (for some users) menstrual-cycle phase.

oura.average_hrv.label: Oura HRV (overnight average)
oura.average_hrv.note: Oura's overnight average HRV; higher than baseline = better recovered. Measured differently from WHOOP HRV, so its baseline is kept separate.

oura.lowest_heart_rate.label: Oura lowest heart rate (overnight)
oura.lowest_heart_rate.note: Oura's overnight low; NOT the same metric as WHOOP resting heart rate, so its baseline is kept separate.
